page: Amazon Web Services x8i.2xlarge is a memory-intensive instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and network bandwidth is rated at a baseline of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows strong single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core scores reaching 2171.0, placing it in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance in the top 25% tier, registering 34.38 GB/sec and 14.52 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core performance is average, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8429.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 8647.15. Memory bandwidth tests show 640.12 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 75.1 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The processor supports Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, contributing to strong text generation speeds of 304.11 tokens/sec for small 135M LLM models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x8i.2xlarge offers a high density of DDR5 memory relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. However, the lack of local storage and a relatively low baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps represent key tradeoffs for data-intensive operations. This instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, text processing, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-intensive instance featuring 8 vCPUs across 4 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB. The instance lacks local storage and has a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2171.0 and strong single-threaded text and compression processing. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. Supported instruction sets include AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which assist in tasks like small-scale LLM text generation. The x8i.2xlarge is suited for memory-bound workloads, database operations, and text processing tasks that benefit from high memory capacity per core without requiring local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-intensive instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-intensive instance with 8 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A memory-intensive x86_64 instance featuring 128 GB of DDR5 RAM and Intel Xeon processors for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 480 MB L3 cache
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage
Suited for database operations, text processing, and memory-bound tasks
